Requirement: IF VehSpd AliveCounter is unchanged for [3 consecutive cycles], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_ALC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_ALC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement uses 'THE SYSTEM' as the system name, which is ambiguous and does not specify the exact component or subsystem responsible for the action. INCOSE guidelines and EARS syntax require clear identification of the system name to ensure unambiguous assignment of responsibility.

Requirement: IF VehSpd > 250 kph, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_OOR_HI_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_OOR_HI - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement specifies a 'transition to safe state' without defining what constitutes a 'safe state' for the system. This ambiguity violates INCOSE guidelines for clarity and testability, as stakeholders cannot verify compliance without explicit criteria for the safe state condition.

Requirement: IF VehSpd is not received within [50 ms], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_TO_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_TO - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement contains multiple system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition) under a single condition, violating the EARS atomicity rule which mandates one requirement per single system response. This creates ambiguity in verification and testability.

Requirement: IF VehSpd AliveCounter is unchanged for [3 consecutive cycles], THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL: - detect F_ALC_VehSpd within [10 ms] - react within [10 ms] - set DTC_101_VSPD_ALC - transition to safe state.
State: Torque = 0 Nm, LKA OFF, Warning ON
Rationale: The requirement follows EARS syntax with clear precondition (VehSpd AliveCounter unchanged for 3 cycles), system name (THE SYSTEM), modality (SHALL), and structured system responses (detect, react, set DTC, transition). Timing constraints (10 ms) are measurable. No ambiguity in trigger or response actions. Complies with INCOSE guidelines for clarity, specificity, and testability.
